# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC for the security group
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet for the EC2 instance
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Existing EC2 KeyPair
  AmiId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Image::Id
    Description: AMI for the EC2 instance
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type
  AppVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Size of the application data volume in GiB
  AppVolumeType:
    Type: String
    Default: gp3
    AllowedValues:
      - gp2
      - gp3
      - io1
      - io2
      - st1
      - sc1
    Description: EBS volume type
  AppVolumeIops:
    Type: Number
    Default: 3000
    MinValue: 100
    Description: IOPS for io1/io2/gp3 volumes
  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Root volume size in GiB
  AllowedSshCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR allowed to SSH to the instance

Conditions:
  IsGp3: !Equals [!Ref AppVolumeType, gp3]
  IsIo1: !Equals [!Ref AppVolumeType, io1]
  IsIo2: !Equals [!Ref AppVolumeType, io2]
  NeedsIops: !Or
    - !Condition IsGp3
    - !Condition IsIo1
    - !Condition IsIo2

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instance
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedSshCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: app-instance-sg

  AppInstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  AppInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: /
      Roles:
        - !Ref AppInstanceRole

  AppInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref AmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref AppInstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: app-instance
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash
          set -euxo pipefail
          DEVICE="/dev/xvdf"
          MOUNT_POINT="/appdata"
          if [ -b "${DEVICE}" ]; then
            if ! blkid "${DEVICE}"; then
              mkfs -t xfs "${DEVICE}"
            fi
            mkdir -p "${MOUNT_POINT}"
            UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value "${DEVICE}")
            grep -q "${UUID}" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=${UUID} ${MOUNT_POINT} xfs defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
            mount -a
            chown ec2-user:ec2-user "${MOUNT_POINT}" || true
          fi

  AppDataVolume:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt AppInstance.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref AppVolumeSize
      VolumeType: !Ref AppVolumeType
      Iops: !If [NeedsIops, !Ref AppVolumeIops, !Ref AWS::NoValue]
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: app-data-volume

  AppDataVolumeAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      Device: /dev/xvdf
      InstanceId: !Ref AppInstance
      VolumeId: !Ref AppDataVolume

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: EC2 instance ID
    Value: !Ref AppInstance
  InstancePrivateIp:
    Description: Private IP of the EC2 instance
    Value: !GetAtt AppInstance.PrivateIp
  DataVolumeId:
    Description: EBS data volume ID
    Value: !Ref AppDataVolume
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
